title: Zip and Print Scores
description: Given two lists: names = ['Alice', 'Bob', 'Charlie'] and scores = [85, 92, 78], use **zip()** to pair them and print each name with its score in the format name: score on separate lines. Expected output: Alice: 85 Bob: 92 Charlie: 78
starter_code:
names = ['Alice', 'Bob', 'Charlie']
scores = [85, 92, 78]

# Use zip() to print name: score for each pair

code_requirements.description: Uses the zip() function to pair names and scores
code_requirements.fail_message: Use zip(names, scores) to pair the lists together instead of printing them one by one.

hints: Loop with: for name, score in zip(names, scores):
Inside the loop, use print(f'{name}: {score}') to format each line
zip() pairs items by position — Alice gets 85, Bob gets 92, Charlie gets 78
